back_translation: And the LORD spoke to Moses in the wilderness of Sinai, in the second year of their coming out from the land of Egypt, in the first month, saying:
"And the sons of Israel shall observe the Passover at its appointed time.
On the fourteenth day of this month, between the two evenings, you shall observe it at its appointed time; according to all its statutes and according to all its ordinances you shall observe it."
And Moses spoke to the sons of Israel to observe the Passover.

And they observed the Passover in the first month, on the fourteenth day of the month, between the two evenings, in the wilderness of Sinai; according to all that the LORD commanded Moses, so the sons of Israel did.
But there were men who were unclean by reason of the corpse of a man, and they could not observe the Passover on that day; and they came near before Moses and before Aaron on that day.
And those men said to him, 'We are unclean by reason of the corpse of a man; why should we be excluded from presenting the offering of the LORD at its appointed time, among the sons of Israel?'
And Moses said to them, 'Stand still, and I will hear what the LORD will command concerning you.'
And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying:
"Speak to the sons of Israel, saying: any man among you or your generations who is unclean by reason of a corpse or on a far journey, he shall observe a Passover to the LORD.
In the second month on the fourteenth day between the two evenings they shall observe it; with unleavened bread and bitter herbs they shall eat it.
They shall not leave any of it until morning, and they shall not break a bone in it; according to all the statute of the Passover they shall observe it.

But the man who is clean and is not on a journey, and refrains from observing the Passover — that soul shall be cut off from his people, because he did not present the offering of the LORD at its appointed time; that man shall bear his sin.

And when a foreigner dwells with you and observes a Passover to the LORD, according to the statute of the Passover and according to its ordinance, so he shall observe it; one statute shall be for you, both for the foreigner and for the native of the land."
And on the day the tabernacle was set up, the cloud covered the tabernacle, the Tent of the Testimony; and in the evening it would be over the tabernacle like the appearance of fire, until morning.
So it was continually: the cloud would cover it, and the appearance of fire by night.
And according to the lifting up of the cloud from above the Tent, and after that the sons of Israel would set out; and in the place where the cloud settled there, there the sons of Israel would camp.
At the word (mouth) of the LORD the sons of Israel set out, and at the word of the LORD they camped; all the days that the cloud settled over the tabernacle they camped.
And when the cloud lingered over the tabernacle many days, the sons of Israel kept the charge of the LORD and did not set out.
And sometimes the cloud would be a number of days over the tabernacle; at the word of the LORD they camped, and at the word of the LORD they set out.
And sometimes the cloud was from evening until morning, and the cloud lifted up in the morning, and they set out; or day and night, and the cloud lifted up, and they set out.
Or two days, or a month, or longer, when the cloud lingered over the tabernacle to settle upon it, the sons of Israel camped and did not set out; and at its lifting up they set out.
At the word of the LORD they camped, and at the word of the LORD they set out; the charge of the LORD they kept, at the word of the LORD through Moses.
